context: Tests whether the agent produces a Rails PR review that applies the correct severity levels (Critical/Suggestion/Nice to have), identifies specific high-risk patterns, follows the defined review area order, and explicitly mandates re-review after Critical findings are fixed.

checklist.name: Correct severity levels used
checklist.description: The review uses exactly the three severity labels Critical, Suggestion, and Nice to have — not custom labels like 'Minor', 'Major', 'Warning', 'Error', or others

checklist.name: permit! flagged Critical
checklist.description: The review identifies the params.require(...).permit! call and assigns it Critical severity

checklist.name: N+1 query identified
checklist.description: The review identifies the N+1 query pattern (loading an association inside a loop without eager loading) and reports it as a finding

checklist.name: Missing index flagged
checklist.description: The review identifies the missing database index on the foreign key column and reports it as a finding

checklist.name: Business logic in controller flagged
checklist.description: The review identifies inline business logic in the controller action (pricing, calculation, or workflow logic) and flags it as a convention violation

checklist.name: Review covers multiple areas
checklist.description: The review explicitly addresses at least four distinct review areas (e.g. Controllers, Queries, Migrations, Security) — not just a flat list of issues without area attribution

checklist.name: Each finding includes mitigation
checklist.description: Every finding in the review includes a concrete remediation suggestion (not just an identification of the problem)

checklist.name: Re-review explicitly required
checklist.description: The review explicitly states that a re-review is required after the Critical findings are addressed — not just 'please fix' but a statement that the reviewer expects to see the diff again

checklist.name: html_safe / raw usage flagged
checklist.description: The review identifies the unsafe use of html_safe or raw on user-supplied content and reports it as a finding (Critical or Suggestion)

checklist.name: Severity action prescribed per level
checklist.description: For each severity level used, the review communicates the required action: Critical items must be fixed before merge, Suggestion items fixed or ticketed, Nice to have items optional
